slides.title: Clean Up After Yourself 🧹
slides.content:
## Leaving Things Tidy

When writing functions, you often open resources like files or database connections. It's crucial to close them when you're done! 

But what if your function has multiple `return` statements or an error occurs? You might forget to close the resource!

slides.title: Enter 'defer' ⏳
slides.content: The `defer` keyword delays the execution of a function until the surrounding function finishes. It guarantees that the deferred code will run, no matter how the function exits.

quizData.question: When does code marked with `defer` actually execute?

slides.title: LIFO Order 🥞
slides.content:
## Multiple Defers

If you use `defer` multiple times in a function, they execute in **LIFO** (Last-In, First-Out) order. Just like adding pancakes to a stack: the last pancake you put on is the first one you eat!
